books.title: The New Organic Grower, 3rd Edition
books.author: Eliot Coleman
books.why: The foundational text for intensive, small-scale production. It focuses on tool efficiency, biological systems, and maximizing output from minimal acreage without heavy machinery.

books.title: The Market Gardener
books.author: Jean-Martin Fortier
books.why: A rigorous manual on making a living from a single acre. It provides specific data on crop profitability, bed layout efficiency, and the 'lean' management of a high-production garden.

books.title: The Lean Farm
books.author: Ben Hartman
books.why: Applies Japanese 'lean' manufacturing principles to agriculture. It is essential for any grower looking to eliminate waste, optimize labor, and increase the financial return on their time.

books.title: The Urban Farmer
books.author: Curtis Stone
books.why: A data-heavy guide to growing high-value crops in small city plots. It prioritizes crops based on 'value per square foot' and 'days to maturity,' which are the core metrics of garden ROI.

books.title: Mini Farming: Self-Sufficiency on 1/4 Acre
books.author: Brett L. Markham
books.why: Includes detailed cost/benefit analyses and yield projections for various crops. It treats the backyard garden as a production unit with measurable inputs and outputs.

books.title: The Year-Round Vegetable Gardener
books.author: Niki Jabbour
books.why: Focuses on 'succession planting' and season extension to ensure the garden produces value 365 days a year. It provides practical timing charts to maximize the utility of every bed.

books.title: How to Grow More Vegetables, 9th Edition
books.author: John Jeavons
books.why: The definitive guide to biointensive gardening. It provides exhaustive data tables on calorie-per-square-foot yields and the economics of sustainable soil building.

books.title: The Small-Scale Poultry Flock, Revised Edition
books.author: Harvey Ussery
books.why: An economic analysis of the 'integrated' homestead. It covers the ROI of poultry not just in eggs/meat, but in their labor for tilling, pest control, and fertilizer production.

youtube.name: Epic Gardening
youtube.why: While high-production, the channel regularly features 'Value of my Garden' segments and variety trials that track total harvest weight and estimated grocery savings.

youtube.name: Self Sufficient Me
youtube.why: Mark Valencia provides excellent data on raised bed yields and 'worth it' rankings for crops based on labor, space, and retail price.

youtube.name: Huw Richards
youtube.why: Focuses on 'Abundance' and intensive systems. His 'Self-Sufficiency' series tracks the calories and volume produced from specific bed sizes over a full season.

youtube.name: No-Till Growers
youtube.why: A technical channel for serious growers. It focuses on soil economics, labor-saving systems, and the efficiency of various mulching and planting techniques.

youtube.name: The Urban Farmer (Curtis Stone)
youtube.why: The channel is almost entirely dedicated to the economics of gardening, focusing on high-value quick-turn crops like microgreens and salad mixes.

youtube.name: Growfully with Jenna
youtube.why: Excellent for backyard growers; she provides detailed breakdowns of seed-starting costs vs. buying starts and the total 'grocery value' of her annual harvests.

youtube.name: The Beginner's Garden
youtube.why: Jill McSheehy tracks yields across multiple years and varieties, providing honest data on which crops are 'ROI losers' due to pests or low yields.

youtube.name: MIgardener
youtube.why: Luke Marion focuses on 'high intensity' gardening - maximizing yield in small spaces through specific spacing and fertilization protocols.

youtube.name: Herb Guy
youtube.why: Suburban herb growing with a focus on basil, rosemary, sage, and other high-value herbs - the crops with the best price-per-pound returns for home growers.

websites.name: USDA ERS - Fruit and Vegetable Prices
websites.why: The gold-standard source for retail price data. This site provides the average price-per-pound for over 150 fruits and vegetables, essential for calculating garden ROI.

websites.name: University of Kentucky Center for Crop Diversification
websites.why: Provides detailed 'Enterprise Budgets' for dozens of vegetable crops, including projected yields, input costs, and labor requirements.

websites.name: Penn State Extension - Vegetable Production
websites.why: Science-backed guides that include commercial-grade yield expectations and cost-of-production data for serious home growers.

websites.name: Johnny's Selected Seeds Calculators
websites.why: Professional tools for calculating seed requirements, succession planting dates, and target harvest windows to ensure continuous production.

websites.name: Cornell Vegetable MD Online
websites.why: Essential for 'protecting the investment.' It provides diagnostic tools to prevent crop loss, which is the biggest threat to garden ROI.

websites.name: The Market Gardener Research Institute
websites.why: Publishes technical articles on small-scale production efficiency, tool comparisons, and crop-specific management for maximum yield.

podcasts.name: The No-Till Market Garden Podcast
podcasts.why: Technical deep-dives into the systems of high-production gardens, with a heavy focus on soil health as an economic asset.

podcasts.name: The Market Gardener Podcast
podcasts.why: Interviews with practitioners who treat gardening as a high-efficiency business, focusing on the numbers that make a plot profitable.

podcasts.name: The Beginner's Garden Podcast
podcasts.why: Despite the name, it is data-driven, featuring annual 'garden audits' where the host reviews her harvest data and economic successes/failures.

podcasts.name: The Urban Farmer Podcast
podcasts.why: An older but vital back catalog focusing specifically on the ROI of different garden crops and small-space production techniques.

social.why: Consistently posts side-by-side 'Trials' (e.g., Dig vs. No Dig) with harvest weights and date-to-maturity data for every crop he grows.
Ben Hartman shares visual examples of 'Lean' gardening: efficient tool storage, waste reduction, and high-speed transplanting systems.
